frame_analyses.description: Needle being loaded onto the needle holder.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper technique for needle loading is observed.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is pierced through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slight deviation from the mark observed.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is pierced through the second mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement is slightly off from the second mark.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of surgeon's knot being tied with a double throw.
Second throw of the knot is being tied with a single throw.
Third throw of the knot being completed with a single throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch noted correctly, throw appears secure.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends being cut to complete the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut is clean and performed inside the trainer.

knot_assessments.notes: Knot is secure with no slipping observed.
Hand switched, knot is completed securely.
Hand switched, knot is completed securely.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Slit closure is complete without visible gaps.

technique_summary: Overall, the intracorporeal suturing and knot tying were performed efficiently with secure knots and minimal suture deviation.
improvement_suggestions: Minimize suture placement deviation for more precise suturing.
Maintain focus on hand stability during knot tying.
strengths: Secure and stable knot tying with appropriate hand switching.
